title: The Fox Without a Tail
lines: A Fox was caught in a trap and lost his tail in escaping.
He was ashamed to show his face to the other foxes.
So he called a meeting of all the foxes and suggested that tails were ugly and unnecessary.
'We would be much better off without them,' he said.
An old Fox stood up and said, 'If you had not lost your tail in misfortune, you would not now be arguing against them.'
And the other foxes agreed and laughed at him.
moral: Do not expect others to adopt your misfortune.
